You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: b139c14d).

## Issue to Resolve

Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE - https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#non-constant_names
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val someMap: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val SOME_MAP: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Create object, with mutable collection property. 

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.50.0

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

You're right. Can you provide a little more context where this variable is declared? Is it at top level, or in a companion object or class?
I have edited the issue, so you can see where the property is declared. 

In my case it is an object's property. I did not check for other cases as top level or companion object's variables (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming should not be applied to class properties according to https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names).

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

 270Ôćĺ## Discouraged comment location

 272ÔćĺDetect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).

 274Ôćĺ!!! note
 275Ôćĺ    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier.

 283Ôćĺ        if (true)
 284Ôćĺ            // some comment
 285Ôćĺ            bar()
 286Ôćĺ    }

 317Ôćĺ## Disallow consecutive comments
 318Ôćĺ
 319ÔćĺConsecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
 320Ôćĺ- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
 321Ôćĺ- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
 322Ôćĺ- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
 323Ôćĺ
 324ÔćĺConsecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
 325Ôćĺ
 326Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 327Ôćĺ
 328Ôćĺ    ```kotlin
 329Ôćĺ    // An EOL comment
 330Ôćĺ    // may be followed by another EOL comment
 331Ôćĺ    val foo = "foo"

 333Ôćĺ    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
 334Ôćĺ
 335Ôćĺ    /*
 336Ôćĺ     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
 337Ôćĺ     */
 338Ôćĺ
 339Ôćĺ    /**
 340Ôćĺ      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
 341Ôćĺ      */
 342Ôćĺ    fun bar() = "bar"
 343Ôćĺ    ```
 344Ôćĺ
 345Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 346Ôćĺ
 347Ôćĺ    ```kotlin
 348Ôćĺ    /*
 349Ôćĺ     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
 350Ôćĺ     */
 351Ôćĺ    /*
 352Ôćĺ     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
 353Ôćĺ     */
 354Ôćĺ    val bar = "bar" 
 355Ôćĺ
 356Ôćĺ    /**
 357Ôćĺ      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
 358Ôćĺ      */
 359Ôćĺ
 360Ôćĺ    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
 361Ôćĺ    ```
 362Ôćĺ
 363ÔćĺRule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
 364Ôćĺ
 365Ôćĺ!!! Note
 366Ôćĺ    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.

 368Ôćĺ## Function literal
 369Ôćĺ
 370ÔćĺEnforces the parameters of a function literal and the arrow to be written on the same line as the opening brace if the maximum line length is not exceeded. In case the parameters are wrapped to multiple lines then this is respected.

 380Ôćĺ                    foo + bar
 381Ôćĺ                }

 383Ôćĺ                { foo: Foo ->
 384Ôćĺ                    foo.repeat(2)
 385Ôćĺ                }
 386Ôćĺ            val foobar4 =
 387Ôćĺ                { foo: Foo, bar: Bar ->
 388Ôćĺ                    foo + bar
 389Ôćĺ                }
 390Ôćĺ            val foobar5 = { foo: Foo, bar: Bar -> foo + bar }
 391Ôćĺ            val foobar6 =
 392Ôćĺ                {
 393Ôćĺ                        foo: Foo,
 394Ôćĺ                        bar: Bar
 395Ôćĺ                    ->
 396Ôćĺ                    foo + bar
 397Ôćĺ                }

 400Ôćĺ    // at the X character on the right           X

 403Ôćĺ                fooooooooooooooo: Foo

 435Ôćĺ## Function signature
 436Ôćĺ
 437ÔćĺRewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)

 508Ôćĺ## If else bracing
 509Ôćĺ
 510ÔćĺIf at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.

 512Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 513Ôćĺ
 514Ôćĺ    ```kotlin
 515Ôćĺ    fun foo(value: int) {
 516Ôćĺ        if (value > 0) {
 517Ôćĺ            doSomething()
 518Ôćĺ        } else if (value < 0) {
 519Ôćĺ            doSomethingElse()
 520Ôćĺ        } else {
 521Ôćĺ            doSomethingElse2()
 522Ôćĺ        }
 523Ôćĺ    }
 524Ôćĺ    ```
 525Ôćĺ
 526Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 527Ôćĺ
 528Ôćĺ    ```kotlin

 530Ôćĺ        if (value > 0)
 531Ôćĺ            doSomething()
 532Ôćĺ        else if (value < 0) {
 533Ôćĺ            doSomethingElse()
 534Ôćĺ        } else
 535Ôćĺ            doSomethingElse2()
 536Ôćĺ    }
 537Ôćĺ    ```
 538Ôćĺ

 544Ôćĺ## If else wrapping
 545Ôćĺ
 546ÔćĺA single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
 547Ôćĺ
 548Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 549Ôćĺ
 550Ôćĺ    ```kotlin
 551Ôćĺ    fun foobar() {
 552Ôćĺ        if (true) foo()
 553Ôćĺ        if (true) foo() else bar()
 554Ôćĺ    }
 555Ôćĺ    ```
 556Ôćĺ
 557Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 558Ôćĺ
 559Ôćĺ    ```kotlin
 560Ôćĺ    fun foobar() {
 561Ôćĺ        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
 562Ôćĺ        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
 563Ôćĺ        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
 564Ôćĺ        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
 565Ôćĺ    }
 566Ôćĺ    ```
 567Ôćĺ
 568ÔćĺRule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 569Ôćĺ
 570Ôćĺ!!! Note
 571Ôćĺ    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 572Ôćĺ
 573Ôćĺ## Naming
 574Ôćĺ
 575Ôćĺ### Function naming
 576Ôćĺ
 577ÔćĺEnforce naming of function. 
 578Ôćĺ
 579Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 580Ôćĺ
 581Ôćĺ    ```kotlin
 582Ôćĺ    fun foo() {}
 583Ôćĺ    fun fooBar() {}
 584Ôćĺ    ```
 585Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint Test"
 586Ôćĺ
 587Ôćĺ    ```kotlin
 588Ôćĺ    @Test
 589Ôćĺ    fun `Some name`() {}
 590Ôćĺ
 591Ôćĺ    @Test
 592Ôćĺ    fun do_something() {}
 593Ôćĺ    ```
 594Ôćĺ
 595Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 596Ôćĺ
 597Ôćĺ    ```kotlin

 599Ôćĺ    fun Foo_Bar() {}

 601Ôćĺ    fun do_something() {}
 602Ôćĺ    ```
 603Ôćĺ
 604Ôćĺ!!! note
 605Ôćĺ    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit`, `org.testng` or `kotlin.test` are considered to be test functions. Functions in such classes are allowed to have underscores in the name. Or function names can be specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention.
 606Ôćĺ
 607ÔćĺThis rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `FunctionName`.

 609ÔćĺRule id: `function-naming` (`standard` rule set)

 618Ôćĺ

1024Ôćĺ### Content receiver wrapping
1025Ôćĺ
1026ÔćĺWraps the content receiver list to a separate line regardless of maximum line length. If the maximum line length is configured and is exceeded, wrap the context receivers and if needed its projection types to separate lines.

Now let me analyze the code more carefully. The issue is that when a property in an object has a mutable collection type (like `MutableMap`, `MutableList`, `MutableSet`), it should NOT be required to use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming because the value can be changed.

Looking at the `PropertyNamingRule.kt` code:
- Line 54-57: Properties with `const` modifier, top-level values, or object values are treated as "const" properties and require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
- Line 126-130: The `isObjectValue()` function checks if a property is in an object and uses `val` keyword

The problem is that the rule doesn't check whether the type is actually immutable. According to the Android style guide, mutable collection types should use lower camel case.

Let me check what types are considered mutable collections:

Now let me implement the fix. The solution is to add a check for mutable collection types before treating a property as a "const" property. I need to:

1. Add a function to check if a property has a mutable collection type
2. Modify the logic to exclude mutable collection types from requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE

  11Ôćĺ## Binary expression wrapping
  12Ôćĺ
  13ÔćĺWraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. 1022Ôćĺ## Wrapping
1023Ôćĺ
1024Ôćĺ### Content receiver wrapping
1025Ôćĺ
1026ÔćĺWraps the content receiver list to a separate line regardless of maximum line length. If the maximum line length is configured and is exceeded, wrap the context receivers and if needed its projection types to separate lines.
1027Ôćĺ
1028Ôćĺ=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"

1032Ôćĺ    context(Foo)
1033Ôćĺ    fun fooBar()
1034Ôćĺ
1035Ôćĺ    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
1036Ôćĺ    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line
1037Ôćĺ    context(
1038Ôćĺ        Fooooooooooooooooooo1,
1039Ôćĺ        Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2
1040Ôćĺ    )
1041Ôćĺ    fun fooBar()
1042Ôćĺ
1043Ôćĺ    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
1044Ôćĺ    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line.
